questions.title: Primary Ballot for Governor
questions.prompt: If the Democratic primary election for Georgia Governor were held today, who would you vote for if the candidates were:

questions.title: Second Choice for Governor
questions.prompt: And, which candidate would be your second choice?

questions.title: Viability for Governor
questions.prompt: No matter who you are currently supporting or if you are undecided at this point, which Democratic candidate do you think would have the best chance to beat the Republican candidate in November?

questions.title: Runoff for Governor
questions.prompt: If there were a run-off election for Governor, for whom would you vote if the candidates were:

modes.label: What Georgians with a view said

response_universe.explainer: In "What Americans with a view said," we remove unsure/no-opinion responses and rescale the remaining responses to sum to 100%.

metadata.label: Soccer Moms
metadata.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
metadata.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

metadata.label: White Working Class
metadata.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

metadata.label: Barstool Conservatives
metadata.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
metadata.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
tooltip.body: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

metadata.label: Disaffected Young Men
metadata.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
metadata.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.title: Disaffected Young Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

metadata.label: Black Women of Faith
metadata.rationale: The most reliable voting bloc in the Democratic coalition. When their turnout dips even slightly, Democrats lose. Media tracks their enthusiasm as a proxy for the health of the entire Democratic operation.

tooltip.title: Black Women of Faith
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Black, Protestant, Democrat
tooltip.body: The most reliable voting bloc in the Democratic coalition. When their turnout dips even slightly, Democrats lose. Media tracks their enthusiasm as a proxy for the health of the entire Democratic operation.

metadata.label: Hispanic Working Class
metadata.rationale: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Hispanic, No college
tooltip.body: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

metadata.label: Evangelical Republicans
metadata.rationale: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

tooltip.title: Evangelical Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Protestant
tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

metadata.label: MAGA Country
metadata.rationale: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

tooltip.title: MAGA Country
tooltip.subtitle: Rural, Republican, White, No college
tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

metadata.label: TikTok Left
metadata.rationale: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

tooltip.title: TikTok Left
tooltip.subtitle: Gen Z, Democrat
tooltip.body: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

metadata.label: Costco Centrists
metadata.rationale: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

tooltip.title: Costco Centrists
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

metadata.label: Tradwifes
metadata.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
metadata.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

tooltip.title: Tradwifes
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

metadata.label: Young Black Men
metadata.rationale: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

tooltip.title: Young Black Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Black, Gen Z
tooltip.body: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

metadata.label: Country Club Republicans
metadata.demographic_composition: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
metadata.rationale: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

tooltip.title: Country Club Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

metadata.label: Latte Liberals
metadata.rationale: The ideological anchor of the Democratic Party. Highly engaged, high-turnout, and the source of most progressive policy energy. When fired up, Democratic campaigns have money and volunteers.

tooltip.title: Latte Liberals
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Urban, Graduate/professional degree
tooltip.body: The ideological anchor of the Democratic Party. Highly engaged, high-turnout, and the source of most progressive policy energy. When fired up, Democratic campaigns have money and volunteers.

tooltip.body: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

metadata.label: Kennedy Catholics
metadata.rationale: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

tooltip.title: Kennedy Catholics
tooltip.subtitle: Catholic, Suburban or Urban
tooltip.body: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

metadata.label: Red State Women
metadata.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
metadata.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

tooltip.title: Red State Women
tooltip.subtitle: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

metadata.label: Boomer Democrats
metadata.demographic_composition: Boomers+, Democrat, College completed or Graduate degree
metadata.rationale: The reliable turnout machine of the Democratic Party — they actually show up in midterms. Often overlooked for flashier demographics, but their consistency keeps Democrats competitive in off-year elections.

tooltip.title: Boomer Democrats
tooltip.subtitle: Boomers+, Democrat, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The reliable turnout machine of the Democratic Party — they actually show up in midterms. Often overlooked for flashier demographics, but their consistency keeps Democrats competitive in off-year elections.

metadata.label: Blue-Collar Men
metadata.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
metadata.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

metadata.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
metadata.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

tooltip.title: GenX Forgotten Middle
tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban
tooltip.body: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

metadata.label: Secular Suburbanites
metadata.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
metadata.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

metadata.label: Poor Rural Democrats
metadata.rationale: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

tooltip.title: Poor Rural Democrats
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Rural, Poor (<35k)
tooltip.body: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

olu_brown.label: White Working Class
olu_brown.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

olu_brown.label: Barstool Conservatives
olu_brown.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
olu_brown.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

amanda_duffy.label: Barstool Conservatives
amanda_duffy.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
amanda_duffy.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
tooltip.body: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

derrick_jackson.label: Disaffected Young Men
derrick_jackson.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
derrick_jackson.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

derrick_jackson.label: Tradwifes

derrick_jackson.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. mike_thurmond.label: Soccer Moms

geoff_duncan.label: Soccer Moms
geoff_duncan.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
geoff_duncan.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. geoff_duncan.label: White Working Class
geoff_duncan.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

geoff_duncan.label: Barstool Conservatives
geoff_duncan.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
geoff_duncan.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

jason_esteves.label: Soccer Moms
jason_esteves.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
jason_esteves.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. jason_esteves.label: White Working Class
jason_esteves.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

jason_esteves.label: Barstool Conservatives
jason_esteves.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
jason_esteves.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

derrick_jackson.label: MAGA Country
derrick_jackson.rationale: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

tooltip.title: MAGA Country
tooltip.subtitle: Rural, Republican, White, No college
tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

derrick_jackson.label: TikTok Left
derrick_jackson.rationale: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

tooltip.title: TikTok Left
tooltip.subtitle: Gen Z, Democrat
tooltip.body: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

derrick_jackson.label: Costco Centrists

derrick_jackson.label: Tradwifes
derrick_jackson.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
derrick_jackson.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

mike_thurmond.label: Soccer Moms
mike_thurmond.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
mike_thurmond.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

mike_thurmond.label: White Working Class
mike_thurmond.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

mike_thurmond.label: Barstool Conservatives
mike_thurmond.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
mike_thurmond.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

keisha_bottoms.label: Soccer Moms
keisha_bottoms.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
keisha_bottoms.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms

keisha_bottoms.label: White Working Class
keisha_bottoms.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

keisha_bottoms.label: Barstool Conservatives
keisha_bottoms.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
keisha_bottoms.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

olu_brown.label: Young Black Men
olu_brown.rationale: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

olu_brown.label: Red State Women

geoff_duncan.label: Soccer Moms
geoff_duncan.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
geoff_duncan.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

geoff_duncan.label: White Working Class
geoff_duncan.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

geoff_duncan.label: Barstool Conservatives
geoff_duncan.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
geoff_duncan.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

geoff_duncan.label: Disaffected Young Men
geoff_duncan.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
geoff_duncan.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

jason_esteves.label: Soccer Moms

jason_esteves.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. jason_esteves.label: White Working Class
jason_esteves.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

jason_esteves.label: Barstool Conservatives
jason_esteves.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
jason_esteves.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

mike_thurmond.label: Soccer Moms
mike_thurmond.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
mike_thurmond.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms

tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

mike_thurmond.label: White Working Class
mike_thurmond.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

mike_thurmond.label: Barstool Conservatives
mike_thurmond.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
mike_thurmond.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

keisha_bottoms.label: Soccer Moms
keisha_bottoms.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
keisha_bottoms.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

keisha_bottoms.label: White Working Class
keisha_bottoms.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

keisha_bottoms.label: Barstool Conservatives
keisha_bottoms.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
keisha_bottoms.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives

keisha_bottoms.label: Disaffected Young Men
keisha_bottoms.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
keisha_bottoms.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

undecided.label: Barstool Conservatives
undecided.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
undecided.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

key_takeaways: This page reports modeled Georgia statewide support across four Democratic gubernatorial questions: primary ballot, second choice, viability, and runoff.
Use the response-universe toggle to compare all Georgians with Georgians who expressed a candidate preference for each question.
Toplines include all four questions, while demographic, crosstab, and archetype panels let you explore one selected question at a time.
content.methodology_note: We estimate a machine learning ensemble that predicts opinion as a function of party, geography, age, sex, education, income, religion, race, and contextual variables. We generate predicted probabilities for detailed demographic cells and post-stratify them to the county, congressional district, state house, and state senate levels using turnout-adjusted population weights before aggregating to the national level. Reported differences (e.g., rural vs. urban) reflect differences in predicted support for those populations as they are composed—including their partisan and demographic makeup—rather than simple unweighted survey averages. As a result, rural–urban differences are not strictly ceteris paribus; they partly reflect differences in partisan composition. For ceteris paribus-style comparisons, we report within-group breakdowns and crosstabs (e.g., rural vs. urban within party).
